# Decisions & Open Questions

> This file is your direct communication channel with the Agentic Team.  
> The Orchestrator consults this file at every Sprint Gate and at the start of each sprint.  
> Fill it in yourself — agents automatically adapt their behavior based on the status below.
>
> **Web UI available:** Run `node .github/webapp/server.js` and open http://127.0.0.1:3000 → **Decisions** tab to view, create, and answer decisions in a visual interface. The web UI writes directly to this file.

---

## How does this file work?

| Column | Explanation |
|-------|--------|
| **ID** | Unique ID, format `DEC-NNN` |
| **Type** | `DECIDED` (you have decided) or `OPEN_QUESTION` (waiting for your answer) |
| **Status** | `OPEN` · `DECIDED` · `DEFERRED` · `EXPIRED` |
| **Priority** | `HIGH` · `MEDIUM` · `LOW` |
| **Scope** | Which phase, agent, or sprint this affects (e.g. `Phase 2`, `SP-3`, `PR/Review Agent`, `All sprints`) |
| **Decision / Question** | What has been decided or what needs to be answered |
| **Your answer / Notes** | Fill this in for OPEN_QUESTION so the Orchestrator can process it |
| **Date** | Date of entry or last update |

**Orchestrator behavior rules:**
- `OPEN` + priority `HIGH` + sprint touches the scope → **Sprint Gate blocks** until you answer
- `OPEN` + priority `MEDIUM/LOW` → Orchestrator reports it but does not block
- `DECIDED` → Orchestrator injects the decision as a hard constraint into all relevant agents
- `DEFERRED` → Orchestrator ignores until date or scope trigger
- `EXPIRED` → Orchestrator ignores entirely

---

## Open Questions (waiting for your answer)

| ID | Priority | Scope | Question | Your answer | Date |
|----|-----------|-------|-------|---------------|-------|

| | | | *(No open questions)* | | |

## Decided Items (agents act on these)

### Transformation Decisions (DEC-T series)

| ID | Priority | Scope | Decision | Notes | Date |
|----|-----------|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| DEC-T-001 | HIGH | All phases | System operates in dual-mode: CREATE (primary) and AUDIT (secondary) | CREATE mode is the default. AUDIT mode is preserved for backward compatibility. Both modes share the same agent pipeline, contracts, and guardrails. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-002 | HIGH | All agents | Every phase agent has explicit CREATE and AUDIT subsections | Each agent skill file contains mode-specific instructions under clearly labeled headers. Agents switch behavior based on `cycle_type` in session state. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-003 | HIGH | Phase 1 | Phase 1 agents design new business models and requirements (CREATE) instead of analyzing existing ones (AUDIT) | Business Analyst defines the business model. Domain Expert maps the problem space. Sales Strategist designs go-to-market. Financial Analyst builds revenue projections. Product Manager produces the product roadmap. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-004 | HIGH | Phase 2 | Phase 2 agents design new architecture (CREATE) instead of auditing existing code | Software Architect designs from scratch using ADRs. Senior Developer defines implementation standards. DevOps plans CI/CD pipelines. Security Architect defines security posture. Data Architect designs data models. Legal Counsel maps compliance requirements. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-005 | HIGH | Phase 3 | Phase 3 agents design new UX/UI (CREATE) instead of evaluating existing interfaces | UX Researcher creates personas and journey maps from market research. UX Designer produces information architecture and wireframes. UI Designer creates visual design system. Accessibility Specialist defines WCAG baseline. Content Strategist designs content model. Localization Specialist plans i18n architecture. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-006 | HIGH | Phase 4 | Phase 4 agents create new brand identity (CREATE) instead of auditing existing brand | Brand Strategist designs brand from positioning through visual identity. Growth Marketer designs acquisition funnels. CRO Specialist designs conversion optimization framework. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-007 | HIGH | Synthesis | Synthesis Agent frames output as "Solution Blueprint" (CREATE) or "Capability Heatmap" (AUDIT) | Master report structure adapts to mode. Cross-Team Blocker Matrix applies to both modes. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-008 | MEDIUM | Onboarding | Onboarding intake adapts to mode: project brief + goals (CREATE) vs. codebase + documentation (AUDIT) | Onboarding Agent detects mode from the command and adjusts its intake questionnaire accordingly. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-009 | HIGH | Session state | Session state contract supports 11 cycle types including CREATE variants | FULL_CREATE, PARTIAL_CREATE, COMBO_CREATE added alongside existing AUDIT variants. FEATURE, REEVALUATE, SCOPE_CHANGE, HOTFIX, REFRESH are mode-agnostic. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-010 | MEDIUM | copilot-instructions | copilot-instructions.md routes CREATE and AUDIT commands through the same pipeline with mode differentiation | Command table in copilot-instructions includes CREATE commands (primary), AUDIT commands (secondary), and on-demand commands (both modes). | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-011 | HIGH | Playbooks | Two separate playbooks: software-creation-playbook.md (CREATE) and commercial-software-audit-playbook.md (AUDIT) | The creation playbook was written from scratch. The audit playbook was preserved as-is for backward compatibility. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-012 | MEDIUM | Contracts, Guardrails | Contracts and guardrails are mode-agnostic with targeted mode-specific sections where needed | analysis-output-contract and sprintplan-output-contract received terminology updates. session-state-contract was rewritten. All guardrails apply universally. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-013 | LOW | Phase 5 | Phase 5 implementation pipeline requires zero changes for dual-mode support | Implementation, Test, PR/Review, Documentation, GitHub Integration, KPI, and Retrospective agents are inherently mode-agnostic — they operate on sprint stories regardless of origin mode. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-014 | LOW | Support agents | Support agents (Reevaluate, Feature, Questionnaire, Scope Change, Brand & Assets, Storybook) require zero changes | These agents operate on structured input independent of the originating mode. | 2025-07-18 |
| DEC-T-015 | MEDIUM | All phases | Critic and Risk agents validate CREATE outputs using the same scoring rubric as AUDIT | Quality gates do not differentiate by mode — the same standards apply to designed solutions as to audited solutions. Critic Agent received minor context updates. | 2025-07-18 |

| DEC-R2-003 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Legal) | License: **MIT**. IP owner: **Robert Agterhuis** (individual). | Source: Q-33-003, Q-33-004. LICENSE file and copyright headers are P0 items in Sprint 1. | 2026-03-08 |
| DEC-R2-004 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Localization) | **English only** — no localization, no i18n infrastructure, no RTL support required. | Source: Q-35-001, Q-35-002. All localization sprint work SUPERSEDED. BLK-003 (i18n approach) SUPERSEDED. ~40 SP removed. | 2026-03-08 |
| DEC-R2-005 | HIGH | All sprints | **Solo developer** — 1 full-time developer, 30 SP per 2-week sprint. Sequential execution only. | Source: Q-05-001, Q-06-001. No parallel Tech+UX tracks. All sprints merged into single sequential track. | 2026-03-08 |
| DEC-R2-006 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Data) | **File-based storage only** — no database migration. All data improvements must work with filesystem. | Source: Q-06-002. Data layer recommendations adjusted to file-based patterns (temp-file-then-rename, JSON schema validation). | 2026-03-08 |

Only 4 LOW/MEDIUM items remain (~11 SP). | Source: reevaluation-report-3.md Section 5. All v2 recommendations closed. 188/188 SP delivered. | 2026-03-08 |
| DEC-R3-002 | HIGH | Phase 2, Phase 3 | **Risk profile confirmed LOW for localhost deployment.** 0 OWASP critical/high, CSP headers, input sanitization, secret scanning. Aggregate risk score 14 (was 93 at v1). | Source: reevaluation-report-3.md Section 6. Agents should not escalate security findings beyond ADVISORY for localhost scope. | 2026-03-08 |

ARIA landmarks provide alternative navigation. | Source: reevaluation-report-3.md NEW-R3-001. Implementation Agent may fix as part of any future frontend work (~1 SP). | 2026-03-08 |

### Operational Decisions

| ID | Priority | Scope | Decision | Notes | Date |
|----|-----------|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| DEC-102 | HIGH | Phase 2 | Infrastructure must be defined entirely using Infrastructure as Code with Bicep | All Azure resources must be provisioned through Bicep templates stored in the repository. Manual Azure Portal provisioning is prohibited except for break-glass recovery scenarios. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-103 | HIGH | Phase 2 (DevOps) | All infrastructure deployments must run through Azure DevOps pipelines using Bicep | CI/CD pipelines perform what-if validation before deployment and enforce gated approvals for production environments. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-104 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Architecture) | Infrastructure architecture must follow Microsoft Well-Architected Framework principles | Architecture decisions must explicitly address Reliability, Security, Cost Optimization, Operational Excellence, and Performance Efficiency. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-105 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (IaC Structure) | Bicep codebase must use modular template architecture | Core modules stored under /infra/modules and composed by environment-level templates. Modules must be reusable and parameterized. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-106 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Security) | All infrastructure deployments must implement secure defaults aligned with Azure security best practices | Includes managed identities, private endpoints where applicable, RBAC least-privilege roles, and Azure Policy compliance enforcement. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-107 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Reliability) | Infrastructure must include built-in resilience patterns defined in Bicep templates | Where applicable: zone redundancy, automatic scaling, health probes, and retry-friendly architecture patterns. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-108 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Cost Optimization) | All Bicep templates must include cost-aware configuration defaults | Examples include right-sized SKUs, autoscaling policies, lifecycle policies for storage, and cost monitoring integration. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-109 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Operational Excellence) | Infrastructure must integrate Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and diagnostics at deployment time | Diagnostic settings and monitoring resources must be created automatically via Bicep modules. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-110 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Governance) | Infrastructure must enforce Azure Policy and tagging standards through Bicep deployment | Required tags include environment, owner, cost center, and application. Policy assignments must be deployed alongside resources. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-111 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Environment Strategy) | Infrastructure environments must follow environment parity using parameterized Bicep deployments | Dev, Test, and Prod environments use the same templates with environment-specific parameter files. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-244 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Parser/Project Boundaries) | What is the required policy for typed ESLint rules using TypeScript project boundaries in monorepos? | Typed linting must use explicit project boundaries/references to avoid cross-package leakage; each package validates only declared source and dependency graph. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-243 | HIGH | Phase 3 (TSConfig Governance) | Must all projects extend approved base `tsconfig` presets with controlled exceptions only? | Yes. All tsconfig files must extend approved base presets; deviations require documented rationale and reviewer approval. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-245 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (ESLint Plugin Governance) | What plugin governance policy is required (approved list, version pinning, security review cadence)? | Maintain an approved plugin allowlist, pin plugin versions, and review plugin security/maintenance status monthly; unapproved plugins require security sign-off. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-246 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Rule Severity Model) | What severity policy is required for stylistic vs correctness rules, and how are warning backlogs governed? | Correctness/security rules are errors (blocking); stylistic rules are warnings initially with tracked backlog and defined promotion-to-error milestones each sprint. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-242 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Import/Module Safety) | Which module/import safety rules are mandatory (`no-cycle`, unresolved imports, type-only imports)? | Enforce unresolved-import checks, circular dependency detection for critical paths, and consistent type-only imports where applicable to reduce runtime overhead. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-249 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Monorepo TS References) | Are TypeScript project references mandatory in monorepos, and what dependency boundary checks are required? | Yes. Use TypeScript project references for build order and isolation, and enforce dependency-boundary checks so packages import only declared/public APIs. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-250 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Path Aliases Policy) | What policy governs `paths` aliases and import roots to avoid runtime mismatch and hidden coupling? | Path aliases must map to real runtime-resolvable paths and be centralized in base config; cross-package deep alias imports are prohibited unless explicitly exported. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-251 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (JS Interop Policy) | What policy applies to `allowJs`/`checkJs` and gradual migration of JavaScript code into TypeScript? | `allowJs` is temporary migration-only; `checkJs` is enabled for remaining JS modules; each quarter requires measurable reduction of untyped JS surface. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-252 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Declaration Output) | What standard is required for declaration files/maps (`declaration`, `declarationMap`) in shared packages? | Shared packages must emit `declaration` and `declarationMap` for consumability/debugging; app-only packages may disable declaration output unless exported externally. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-253 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Public API Typing) | What guarantees are required for exported type contracts (breaking-change detection, semver compatibility)? | Public type contracts require API compatibility checks in CI and semver discipline; breaking type changes require major version bump and migration notes. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-254 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Suppression Governance) | What governance is required for ESLint disables and TypeScript suppressions (expiry, owner, review cadence)? | Every suppression must include rationale, owner, and expiry/ticket reference; suppressions are reviewed monthly and expired suppressions fail CI. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-255 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Generated Code Exemptions) | What lint/typecheck exemption policy applies to generated files and external vendored code? | Generated and vendored code may be excluded from style rules but must pass minimum security/type sanity checks where feasible; generators are linted at source templates. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-256 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Testing Type Safety) | What type-safety standards apply to test code and test utilities versus production code? | Test code follows strict typing with limited pragmatic relaxations; shared test utilities must meet production-level typing to avoid unsafe reuse. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-257 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Upgrade Cadence) | What cadence is required for TypeScript/ESLint major and minor upgrades, including deprecation remediation windows? | Apply monthly minor/patch updates and evaluate major upgrades each quarter; complete deprecation remediation within two release cycles after warning introduction. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-258 | LOW | Phase 3 (Editor Tooling Baseline) | What minimum editor/tooling settings are required for contributors (format-on-save, ESLint extension, TS SDK pinning)? | Recommend format-on-save, ESLint integration, and workspace TypeScript SDK pinning; provide shared editor settings file for consistency. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-259 | LOW | Phase 3 (Autofix Workflow) | What policy governs automatic lint fixes in CI vs local development? | Autofix runs locally/pre-commit; CI validates clean state and does not push code changes automatically to protected branches. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-260 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (CI Failure Triage) | What triage workflow is required for persistent lint/typecheck failures (owner assignment, SLA, escalation)? | Persistent failures require owner assignment within 1 business day, fix SLA by severity, and escalation to engineering lead when SLA is breached. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-261 | HIGH | All phases (Identity & Access) | What end-to-end identity and authorization model is mandatory for users, services, and platform operations? | Standardize on Microsoft Entra ID for workforce identities, federated workload identities for services, least-privilege RBAC/ABAC, and periodic access reviews with automated deprovisioning. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-262 | HIGH | Phase 2, Phase 3 (API Governance) | What API lifecycle governance is required for contract versioning, compatibility, and deprecation? | All APIs require versioned contracts, backward compatibility by default, automated contract tests in CI, and formal deprecation windows with migration guidance before breaking changes. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-263 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Network & Edge) | What Azure network/edge architecture baseline is required for ingress, segmentation, and external exposure? | Use hub-spoke or equivalent segmented topology, private endpoints by default, controlled ingress via WAF-enabled edge, restricted egress, and DNS/private DNS governance. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-264 | HIGH | All phases (Data Governance) | What application-level data governance model is required for classification, encryption, retention, and ownership? | Data assets must be classified, encrypted in transit/at rest, mapped to named data owners, and governed by retention/purge policies with auditable enforcement. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-265 | HIGH | All phases (Business Continuity) | What workload-level continuity and disaster recovery standard is mandatory beyond infra resilience? | Define per-service RTO/RPO, failover strategy (single/multi-region), backup/restore drills, and at least biannual DR game days with remediation tracking. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-266 | MEDIUM | All phases (SRE Operating Model) | What SRE operating model is required for SLOs, error budgets, incident severity, and escalation? | Every critical service must define SLOs and error budgets, adopt a standard incident severity taxonomy, and follow documented escalation/communication playbooks. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-267 | MEDIUM | Phase 5 (Non-Functional Testing) | What non-functional test strategy is mandatory for performance, resilience, and regression prevention? | Require load/performance tests for critical paths, resilience/failure-mode tests for key dependencies, and release-blocking performance regression thresholds in CI/CD. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-268 | HIGH | All phases (Security Assurance) | What cadence is required for threat modeling, security validation, and remediation governance? | Threat modeling is required for new high-impact features, annual penetration testing for internet-facing surfaces, and risk-based remediation SLAs with tracked closure evidence. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-269 | MEDIUM | Phase 4, Phase 5 (Release Lifecycle) | What product/release lifecycle governance is required for feature flags, versioning, and decommissioning? | Enforce semantic versioning, feature-flag ownership with expiry dates, release notes for all production changes, and formal sunset plans for deprecated capabilities. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-270 | MEDIUM | All phases (Documentation & ADRs) | What documentation governance baseline is required for architecture, operations, and decision traceability? | Maintain ADRs for major technical decisions, service/runbook documentation as release prerequisites, and quarterly doc ownership reviews to prevent stale guidance. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-116 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Module Lifecycle) | What module/versioning strategy is required for Bicep modules (registry/template-spec usage, semantic versioning, deprecation policy)? | Use private Bicep registry with semantic versioning; consumers must pin exact module versions; deprecations require migration guidance and a minimum 2-release grace period before removal. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-118 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Naming & Metadata) | What mandatory naming convention and metadata/tag schema must every module enforce beyond current minimum tags? | Enforce CAF-aligned naming plus required tags: environment, owner, costCenter, application, dataClassification, criticality, and managedBy; deployment fails when required metadata is missing. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-135 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Approvals & Separation of Duties) | What environment approval model is required, and which roles are explicitly forbidden from self-approving production deployments? | Production requires 2 approvers from Ops/SRE and Security; requester, author, and committer cannot self-approve; emergency override must be logged and reviewed within 24 hours. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-136 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Service Connections) | Must service connections use workload identity federation only, with PAT/service-principal secrets prohibited by default? | Yes. Use workload identity federation only; PAT and client-secret service connections are prohibited by default; exceptions require security waiver with 30-day expiry. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-137 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Pipeline Secrets) | What is the mandatory secret management pattern for pipelines (Key Vault-backed variables, masking, rotation cadence, zero secret echo)? | Secrets only via Key Vault-backed variable groups; no plaintext pipeline variables; output masking required; rotate credentials every 90 days; fail pipeline on secret echo detection. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-138 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Agent Strategy) | What agent policy applies (Microsoft-hosted vs self-hosted), and what hardening/patching baseline is mandatory for self-hosted agents? | Default to Microsoft-hosted agents; self-hosted agents allowed only when justified, must be hardened, ephemeral where possible, and patched on at least a weekly cadence. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-139 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Quality Gates) | Which test gates are mandatory before deployment (unit/integration/e2e coverage thresholds and fail criteria)? | Block deployment unless unit tests pass with minimum 80% line coverage, integration tests pass for changed services, and post-deploy smoke tests pass. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-140 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Security Gates) | Which security scans are mandatory and blocking (SAST, SCA, secret scan, container scan, IaC scan)? | Blocking gates: SAST high/critical findings must be zero; SCA critical must be zero; secret scan findings must be zero; container high/critical must be zero; IaC policy violations must be zero for production. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-141 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Artifact Integrity) | What artifact controls are required (immutable versioning, provenance, signing, and SBOM publication)? | Artifacts must be immutable and content-addressable, include provenance metadata, be signed before promotion, and publish SBOM for each release artifact. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-142 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Release Strategy) | Which release pattern is mandated for production (rolling, blue-green, canary), and what rollback trigger rules are required? | Production uses blue-green deployments with automated health checks and 15-minute bake time; auto-rollback on health check failure or SLO breach; manual rollback available to on-call. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-239 | HIGH | Phase 3 (TypeScript Strictness) | What TypeScript strictness baseline is mandatory across projects (`strict`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`)? | Require `strict: true`; enable `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` and `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` by default for new/actively maintained TypeScript code. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-240 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Typecheck Gate) | Must `tsc --noEmit` (or equivalent project typecheck) be a required blocking CI check on protected branches? | Yes. Typecheck is mandatory and blocking on protected branches; production release requires successful full-project typecheck. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-241 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Unsafe Escape Hatches) | What policy applies to `any`, `@ts-ignore`, and `@ts-expect-error` usage? | `any` and `@ts-ignore` are prohibited by default; `@ts-expect-error` requires inline justification and is periodically audited for removal. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-247 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Prettier Integration) | What integration model is required between ESLint and Prettier (eslint-config-prettier, formatter ownership)? | Prettier owns formatting and ESLint owns code-quality/correctness; use eslint-config-prettier to avoid conflicts and enforce formatting via pre-commit and CI checks. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-248 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Performance of Lint/Typecheck) | What performance standards are required for lint/typecheck in CI (caching, incremental mode, parallelism thresholds)? | Use dependency/tsbuild caches, incremental typecheck where valid, and parallel execution by package; alert when lint/typecheck duration regresses by over 20%. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-238 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Lint Merge Gate) | What lint policy is blocking for merge (errors/warnings, changed-files scope, autofix expectations)? | Lint errors are blocking in CI; warnings are non-blocking but tracked; changed-files lint is required on PR and full-repo lint runs on main/release. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-237 | HIGH | Phase 3 (ESLint Baseline) | Must all JavaScript/TypeScript packages use a single shared ESLint baseline configuration with drift prohibited by default? | Yes. Use a shared repo-level ESLint baseline (flat config preferred) with package-level overrides only by approved exception. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-236 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Incident & Recovery) | What incident response and recovery expectations apply when GitHub Actions or runners are degraded? | Maintain CI incident runbook with fallback procedures, define RTO targets for critical pipelines, notify stakeholders during degradation, and perform post-incident review with actions. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-235 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Security Scanning Depth) | What minimum CodeQL/secret/dependency scanning depth and language coverage is required in CI? | Enable CodeQL for all supported repository languages, mandatory secret scanning and dependency scanning on PR and default branch, with high/critical findings blocking merge. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-234 | LOW | Phase 2 (Local CI Simulation) | Is local workflow simulation (e.g., `act` or equivalent) required or optional for developer workflows? | Local CI simulation is optional but recommended for rapid feedback; canonical gating remains GitHub-hosted CI results on pull requests. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-228 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Dependency Automation) | What Dependabot/Renovate policy is required for update cadence, grouping, and auto-merge constraints? | Run dependency updates weekly, group low-risk updates by ecosystem, and allow auto-merge only when all required checks pass and change is patch-level with no policy violations. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-229 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Flaky Test Governance) | What workflow is required for flaky test quarantine, ownership, SLA, and merge-block behavior? | Flaky tests must be auto-flagged, assigned an owner within 1 business day, fixed or quarantined within 7 days, and cannot remain quarantined beyond one sprint without approval. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-230 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Monorepo CI Scope) | What path-filter and affected-project strategy is required to optimize CI in monorepos while preserving safety? | Use path-filtered and affected-project workflows for PRs plus periodic full-repo validation; any shared infra/build-config changes trigger full CI matrix automatically. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-231 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Release Workflow Controls) | What environment protection and approval controls are required for release/deploy workflows in GitHub? | Production environments require protected environment rules, named approvers, required checks, deployment wait timer where needed, and immutable artifact input only. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-232 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Workflow Change Control) | What review policy is mandatory for `.github/workflows` and shared action changes (CODEOWNERS, approvals, change windows)? | Enforce CODEOWNERS on workflow/action paths, minimum two approvals including platform owner, and scheduled change windows for production-impacting workflow changes. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-233 | LOW | Phase 2 (Workflow Naming & Docs) | What naming/documentation standard is required for workflows, jobs, and runbook references? | Workflows/jobs must use consistent descriptive naming convention and include purpose, triggers, owners, and runbook links in repo docs for operational clarity. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-219 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Workflow Trigger Safety) | What trigger restrictions are required for sensitive workflows (workflow_dispatch, pull_request_target, fork behavior)? | Sensitive workflows cannot run privileged steps on untrusted fork code; `pull_request_target` use requires security review and strict path/event guards. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-221 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Reusable Workflow Architecture) | What reusable workflow/composite action strategy is required across repositories? | Use centrally maintained reusable workflows for common CI/CD stages and minimal composite actions for atomic tasks; pin versions by immutable ref and version changes via reviewed PRs. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-222 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Runner Strategy) | What policy governs GitHub-hosted vs self-hosted runners, including hardening and network isolation for self-hosted? | Default to GitHub-hosted runners; self-hosted runners require documented need, isolated network segment, ephemeral execution where possible, hardened baseline, and weekly patching. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-223 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Caching Policy) | What dependency/build cache strategy is required (keys, restore policy, poisoning protection, retention)? | Use deterministic cache keys with lockfile/hash inputs, scoped restore keys, no cache writes from untrusted forks, and retention tuned to 7-30 days by workload churn. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-224 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Concurrency Control) | What concurrency/cancel-in-progress rules apply to prevent conflicting workflow runs and wasted minutes? | Configure workflow/job concurrency groups per branch/environment with cancel-in-progress for non-release branches; production deploy groups must serialize and never overlap. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-225 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Matrix Strategy) | What standards apply to test/build matrix design (OS/runtime coverage, fail-fast, required matrix axes)? | Matrix must cover supported runtime versions and primary OS targets; use fail-fast for non-critical jobs and require full matrix pass for release branches. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-226 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (CI Observability) | What telemetry and alerting baseline is required for workflow health, failure rates, queue times, and MTTR? | Track workflow SLIs (success rate, lead time, queue time, MTTR), publish weekly trends, and alert on sustained failure spikes or queue delays beyond agreed thresholds. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-227 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Retention & Evidence) | What retention policy applies to logs, test reports, artifacts, and security reports produced by workflows? | Retain release artifacts and security evidence for at least 365 days; retain non-release logs/artifacts for 90 days with automated cleanup and legal-hold exceptions. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-210 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (NextJS Performance Budgets) | What Core Web Vitals and bundle budgets are mandatory, and what thresholds block release? | Enforce Core Web Vitals targets at p75 (LCP <= 2.5s, INP <= 200ms, CLS <= 0.1) and route bundle budgets with release block on sustained regressions. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-211 | LOW | Phase 3 (Preview/Feature Flags) | What policy governs preview environments and feature flags for NextJS releases? | Every PR gets ephemeral preview where feasible; feature flags require owner, default-off for risky changes, rollout plan, and cleanup deadline after full rollout. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-212 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (NextJS Upgrade Policy) | What upgrade cadence and support policy applies for NextJS major/minor versions and deprecations? | Apply monthly minor/patch updates and plan major upgrades within 90 days of stable release; deprecated APIs require migration plans and tracked deadlines. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-213 | HIGH | Phase 2 (GitHub Actions Standard) | Must all CI/CD automation be defined as versioned GitHub workflow YAML in-repo, with legacy/manual job runners prohibited except break-glass? | Yes. All automation must run through in-repo GitHub workflow YAML; manual/legacy execution is break-glass only with incident ticket and back-port to workflow within 1 business day. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-214 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Action Supply Chain) | Must all third-party GitHub Actions be pinned to full commit SHA and sourced only from approved publishers? | Yes. Actions must be pinned to full commit SHA; unpinned tags are prohibited in protected branches; third-party actions require approved publisher and periodic review. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-215 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Workflow Permissions) | What minimum GITHUB_TOKEN permission policy applies to workflows and jobs? | Default `permissions: read-all`; elevate per job to least privilege only when required; write permissions on default branch require explicit owner-reviewed justification. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-216 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Identity & Secrets) | Must deployments use OIDC federation to cloud providers with long-lived cloud credentials prohibited in repository secrets? | Yes. Use OIDC federation for cloud auth; long-lived cloud credentials in repository/org secrets are prohibited except temporary approved emergency exception. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-217 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Required Checks) | Which CI checks are mandatory and blocking for merge to protected branches? | Blocking checks: build, unit/integration tests, lint/format, dependency vulnerability scan, and required code scanning results for changed code. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-218 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Branch Protection) | What branch protection baseline is mandatory (PR-only merges, review count, up-to-date requirement, force-push restrictions)? | Enforce PR-only merges, minimum 1 CODEOWNER review, up-to-date branch requirement, no force-push/delete on protected branches, and linear history or merge queue. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-220 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Artifact Integrity) | Must build artifacts include provenance/attestations and be immutable before release promotion? | Yes. Release artifacts must be immutable, traceable to commit/workflow run, and include provenance attestations before promotion. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-198 | LOW | Phase 3 (Vite Dev Proxy Policy) | What standards apply for Vite dev-server proxy usage and local API mocking? | Dev proxy targets must be explicit and environment-scoped; local mocks are versioned test fixtures and must not bypass auth/security logic in integration testing. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-197 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Vite Testing Strategy) | What mandatory test stack/gates apply to Vite apps (unit/component/e2e thresholds)? | Require unit/component tests for changed UI logic, critical-path e2e tests, and minimum 80% line coverage on app code before merge. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-199 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Vite Release Governance) | What release artifact and rollback requirements apply for Vite frontend deployments? | Release immutable versioned artifacts only, keep previous deployable artifact available, and support one-click rollback with smoke-test validation after rollback. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-200 | HIGH | Phase 3 (NextJS Framework Baseline) | Which NextJS architecture baseline is mandatory (App Router, React Server Components policy, pages/legacy usage)? | App Router is the default baseline for new NextJS code; legacy pages usage is allowed only for existing modules with migration plan and owner. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-201 | HIGH | Phase 3 (NextJS Rendering Strategy) | What rendering model policy is required per route (SSR, SSG, ISR, CSR), and who decides exceptions? | Route-level rendering model must be explicitly declared by feature team; default is static/ISR where possible, SSR only when required by data freshness or personalization. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-203 | HIGH | Phase 3 (NextJS Image & Asset Security) | What image optimization and external asset policy is required (allowed domains, optimization mode, CSP alignment)? | Configure strict allowlist for external image domains, keep image optimization enabled in production, and align image/asset sources with CSP policy. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-204 | HIGH | Phase 3 (NextJS Caching & Revalidation) | What caching and revalidation policy is mandatory for fetch/data layers (cache tags, revalidate windows, invalidation rules)? | Define explicit cache and revalidation per data source; use tag/path invalidation for content changes; avoid uncached SSR by default unless required. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-202 | HIGH | Phase 3 (NextJS Data & Secrets Boundary) | What mandatory boundary applies for server components/actions versus client components, especially for secret access? | Secrets and privileged API calls are server-only (server components/actions/route handlers); client components cannot access secrets or privileged tokens directly. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-206 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (NextJS API Route Standards) | What standards apply to route handlers/API routes (validation, error contract, rate limiting, timeout)? | API routes require schema validation, standardized error contract, rate limiting for public endpoints, and explicit timeout/cancellation handling. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-207 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (NextJS Runtime Target) | What runtime policy applies for Node vs Edge execution, and what constraints govern Edge adoption? | Default runtime is Node.js; Edge runtime is allowed only for stateless low-latency use cases after compatibility and observability requirements are met. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-208 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (NextJS Observability) | What observability baseline is mandatory for NextJS apps (structured logs, tracing, Web Vitals, alerting)? | Require structured logs, distributed tracing, Web Vitals collection, and alerting on error-rate/latency thresholds across server and client telemetry. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-209 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (NextJS Testing Gates) | What mandatory test gates apply before merge and release for NextJS features? | Require unit/integration tests for changed logic, critical user journey e2e tests, and minimum 80% line coverage for modified NextJS modules. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-205 | HIGH | Phase 3 (NextJS Security Baseline) | Which mandatory security controls are required in NextJS (security headers, CSRF protection, cookie policy, middleware hardening)? | Enforce security headers, CSRF protections for state-changing endpoints, secure/HttpOnly/SameSite cookies, and reviewed middleware rules for auth and redirects. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-186 | LOW | Phase 2 (Local Developer Containers) | Is a standardized local container/development profile required (devcontainer/compose profile parity with CI)? | Yes. Maintain a standard devcontainer/compose profile aligned with CI dependencies to reduce environment drift and onboarding time. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-187 | LOW | Phase 2 (Image Optimization) | Which optimization techniques are required or forbidden (distroless, trimming, Alpine/Ubuntu policy, debug symbols)? | Prefer minimal secure base images and multi-stage optimization; keep debug symbols out of runtime images; Alpine use requires compatibility validation for native dependencies. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-188 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Exception Governance) | What exception workflow is required for Docker policy deviations (owner, justification, expiry, review cadence)? | Exceptions require owner, risk justification, compensating controls, and max 90-day expiry; security review occurs at approval and monthly until closure. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-189 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Vite Framework Boundary) | What is the approved usage boundary for Vite in this project (SPA tooling only, library builds, or full app runtime)? | Vite is approved for SPA and frontend package/library builds; SSR workloads are not implemented in Vite and must use NextJS when server rendering is required. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-190 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Vite Runtime Baseline) | What Node.js and package-manager baseline is mandatory for Vite projects in CI and local development? | Use pinned active LTS Node.js version in CI and local development with lockfile-enforced installs; package manager must be consistent across repo and enforced in CI. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-191 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Vite Environment Secrets) | What environment variable policy applies for Vite apps, including exposure of client variables? | Client-exposed values must use VITE_ prefix and contain no secrets; secrets remain server-side only and are injected at deployment platform level, never committed. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-192 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Vite Build & Deploy) | What production build/deploy standard is required for Vite artifacts (hashed assets, sourcemaps, immutable cache strategy)? | Production builds must emit content-hashed assets, immutable cache headers for hashed files, and protected sourcemaps (restricted or disabled in public production). | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-193 | HIGH | Phase 3 (Vite Security Headers) | Which mandatory browser security controls must be enforced for Vite-hosted apps (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)? | Enforce CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options/Frame-Ancestors controls, Referrer-Policy, and X-Content-Type-Options at edge/server layer for all production environments. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-194 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Vite Plugin Governance) | What plugin governance model is required for Vite (approved plugin list, review process, update cadence)? | Maintain an approved plugin allowlist; new plugins require security and maintenance review; plugin updates are reviewed monthly with pinned versions in lockfile. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-195 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Vite Target & Polyfills) | What browser target baseline and polyfill strategy is required for Vite builds? | Define browserslist baseline for supported evergreen browsers; polyfills are explicit and minimal, automatically validated in CI compatibility tests. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-196 | MEDIUM | Phase 3 (Vite Performance Budgets) | What bundle-size and runtime performance budgets are mandatory for Vite applications? | Enforce bundle budgets in CI (initial JS and CSS size caps) and block merges on regression above 10% unless approved with mitigation plan. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-178 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Registry Governance) | What registry governance controls are required (immutability, retention, replication, access model)? | Enable repository immutability for release tags, least-privilege RBAC, retention/cleanup policies, and geo-replication for production-critical registries. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-179 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Runtime Hardening) | Which runtime hardening controls are mandatory by default (read-only filesystem, tmpfs, capability drops, seccomp/apparmor)? | Default hardening requires read-only root filesystem, dropped capabilities, restricted seccomp profile, and tmpfs for writable temp paths where needed. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-180 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Network Policy) | What default network exposure policy applies to containers (ingress restrictions, internal-only services, egress controls)? | Apply deny-by-default ingress/egress policy, expose only required ports through approved entry points, and enforce egress allowlists for external dependencies. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-181 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Resource Governance) | What default resource constraints are required (CPU/memory limits, PIDs limits, ulimits) for all workloads? | All workloads must define CPU/memory requests and limits, PIDs limits, and sane ulimit defaults; deployments without constraints are blocked. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-182 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Health & Lifecycle) | What mandatory healthcheck, startup, graceful shutdown, and restart policy standards apply to containers? | Require startup, readiness, and liveness checks where supported, graceful shutdown handling with explicit timeout, and restart policies aligned to workload type. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-183 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Logging & Telemetry) | What container logging and telemetry standard is required (structured logs, correlation IDs, log drivers, retention)? | Use structured JSON logs with correlation IDs to standard output, centralized log collection, and retention aligned with operational and compliance requirements. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-184 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (State & Volumes) | What policy governs stateful container usage (ephemeral rootfs, volume encryption, backup/restore requirements)? | Prefer stateless containers; any persistent volume must be encrypted, have defined backup/restore policy, and periodic restore validation. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-185 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Patch Cadence) | What patch/update SLA applies to container base images and runtime dependencies by severity? | Patch SLA: Critical within 48 hours, High within 7 days, Medium within 30 days, Low within 90 days, with tracked exceptions only by approved waiver. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-165 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Container Runtime) | What base image/runtime policy is required for .NET services (image family, patch cadence, CVE response SLA)? | Use official Microsoft .NET runtime images (non-preview) with monthly patch refresh; Critical CVEs remediated within 48 hours and High within 7 days. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-166 | LOW | Phase 2 (AOT/Trimming) | Is Native AOT or trimming required/allowed, and what compatibility test requirements apply before adoption? | AOT/trimming is optional and allowed by exception; adoption requires compatibility validation, startup/perf benchmark evidence, and rollback plan before production use. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-167 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Support & Deprecation) | What support lifecycle and deprecation policy is required for internal libraries/packages? | Internal packages require at least 12 months support from latest release; deprecations require notice in release notes and a migration window of minimum 2 minor releases. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-168 | LOW | Phase 2 (Developer Experience) | Which local developer workflow standards are mandatory (dotnet format, pre-commit hooks, local test command baseline)? | Require dotnet format, pre-commit lint/test hooks, and a documented one-command local validation workflow matching CI baseline checks. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-169 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Dockerfile Standard) | What mandatory Dockerfile baseline is required for all services (multi-stage, deterministic builds, no `latest` tags)? | Mandatory multi-stage builds, deterministic build args, and pinned image tags/digests; `latest` tag is prohibited in all Dockerfiles and compose files. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-170 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Base Image Policy) | Which base images/registries are approved, and must all base images be pinned by digest? | Use only approved Microsoft/official base images from approved registries; base images must be pinned by digest and updated through controlled PRs. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-171 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Container Privilege Model) | Must all production containers run as non-root with least privilege, and which exceptions are allowed? | Yes. Production containers run as non-root, with dropped Linux capabilities and `no-new-privileges`; exceptions require security waiver and expiry date. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-172 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Secrets Handling) | What is the mandatory secret policy for Docker build and runtime (BuildKit secrets, env var restrictions, mounted secrets)? | Use BuildKit secret mounts for build-time secrets and platform secret stores for runtime; no secrets in Dockerfile, image layers, or committed compose/env files. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-173 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Image Security Gates) | Which container security scans are mandatory/blocking before merge and deployment? | Blocking gates: Critical/High vulnerabilities must be zero in runtime images; secret scan findings must be zero; license policy violations block release. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-174 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Supply Chain Integrity) | Are signed images, SBOM generation, and provenance attestations mandatory for release images? | Yes. Release images must be signed, include SBOM artifacts, and publish provenance attestations; unsigned images cannot be promoted to production. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-175 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Build Strategy) | What build strategy is required for reproducibility and speed (BuildKit cache policy, remote cache usage, rebuild triggers)? | Use BuildKit with reproducible build settings and remote cache; trigger rebuilds on base image, dependency, or Dockerfile changes; avoid non-deterministic build steps. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-176 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Multi-Architecture) | Is multi-arch image publishing required (amd64/arm64), and what validation matrix is mandatory? | Publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 images for production workloads unless explicitly exempted; run smoke tests on both architectures before release. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-177 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Tagging & Versioning) | What image tagging convention is required (semver, commit SHA, environment tags), and which tags are immutable? | Tag images with semver and git SHA; release tags are immutable, environment tags are pointers managed by deployment automation only. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-155 | HIGH | Phase 2 (NuGet Vulnerability Policy) | What vulnerability severity thresholds block PR merge and release for direct/transitive packages? | Block merge/release on any Critical or High vulnerability; Medium requires tracked remediation plan with due date; exceptions require security waiver. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-157 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (API Compatibility) | What backward-compatibility and versioning policy is required for public APIs and contracts? | Public APIs follow semantic versioning; non-breaking changes only in minor/patch releases; breaking changes require major version and published migration notes before release. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-158 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Serialization Policy) | Which serialization defaults are required (System.Text.Json options, casing, enum handling, date/time format, strictness)? | Standardize on System.Text.Json with camelCase properties, string enums, ISO-8601 UTC timestamps, and strict unknown-field handling for internal contracts unless explicitly versioned. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-159 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Async & Resilience) | What async/cancellation/timeout standards are mandatory for I/O and external calls? | All I/O must be async; public async methods require CancellationToken unless technically infeasible; outbound calls must define explicit timeout and retry policy. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-160 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Error Handling) | What exception-handling contract is required for APIs and background jobs (ProblemDetails, correlation IDs, retryability classification)? | APIs must return standardized ProblemDetails; errors must include correlation IDs in logs; transient vs non-transient classification must drive retry behavior. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-161 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Observability) | What logging/metrics/tracing baseline is mandatory for .NET services (structured logs, OpenTelemetry, redaction rules)? | Require structured logging with correlation IDs, OpenTelemetry traces/metrics enabled by default, and mandatory redaction of secrets/PII in logs. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-162 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Testing Gates) | What mandatory test gates apply for C# code before merge/deploy (unit/integration coverage thresholds and smoke checks)? | Block merge unless unit tests pass with minimum 80% line coverage; changed integration tests must pass; deployment requires post-deploy smoke test pass. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-163 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Performance Standards) | What performance budgets and benchmark requirements are mandatory for critical paths? | Define SLO-backed latency/error budgets per critical endpoint; benchmark critical paths before release and block if p95 latency regresses by more than 10% without approved waiver. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-164 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Secure Coding) | Which secure coding controls are mandatory in C# (no custom crypto, input validation, safe deserialization, parameterized queries)? | No custom cryptography; validate all external input; use safe serializers and parameterized data access; security-critical changes require security review before merge. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-148 | LOW | Phase 2 (Efficiency & Cost) | What controls are required to manage pipeline runtime cost and performance (caching, parallelism caps, timeout defaults)? | Enable dependency/build caching, define stage timeout defaults, cap parallelism per agent pool, and monitor cost-per-pipeline with monthly optimization review. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-149 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Audit Evidence) | What mandatory audit evidence must each release preserve (approvals, test/security reports, deployment logs), and for how long? | Preserve approvals, test results, security scan reports, and deployment logs per release for minimum 1 year in tamper-evident storage. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-150 | HIGH | Phase 2 (.NET Runtime Baseline) | Must all new services and libraries target .NET 10 (`net10.0`) by default, with multi-targeting allowed only by exception? | Yes. Default target is net10.0 for all new code; multi-targeting requires documented compatibility need and explicit approval. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-151 | HIGH | Phase 2 (SDK Pinning) | What SDK pinning policy applies for builds (global.json version, roll-forward rules, preview SDK usage)? | Use global.json pinned to approved .NET 10 SDK patch; roll-forward limited to patch updates; preview SDKs are prohibited in CI and production branches. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-152 | HIGH | Phase 2 (C# Compiler Strictness) | What mandatory compiler defaults are required (nullable, implicit usings, language version, warnings as errors)? | Mandatory: nullable enabled, implicit usings enabled, language version set to latest major shipped with pinned SDK, and warnings treated as errors in CI. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-153 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Analyzers & Code Quality) | Which analyzer/rule set is mandatory and blocking for merge (Microsoft analyzers, custom rules, suppression policy)? | Microsoft .NET analyzers are mandatory and blocking; suppressions require inline justification and reviewer approval; new warnings cannot be introduced. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-154 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Dependency Management) | Is Central Package Management with lock files required, and what is the dependency update cadence? | Yes. Use Central Package Management and NuGet lock files in CI; dependency update cadence is weekly for non-breaking updates and monthly review for minor/major upgrades. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-156 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Solution Architecture) | Which project architecture convention is mandatory for C# solutions (layering boundaries, shared kernel rules, anti-corruption boundaries)? | Enforce clean layered architecture (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API) with strict dependency direction inward; shared kernel minimized; external integrations isolated behind anti-corruption adapters. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-143 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Deployment Concurrency) | What deployment lock/concurrency rules apply to prevent overlapping changes in shared environments? | Enforce one active deployment per target environment with queueing/locking; superseded non-production runs may be auto-cancelled, production runs cannot overlap. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-144 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline Observability) | What telemetry/alerting baseline is required for pipeline health, failure notification, and MTTR reporting? | Capture pipeline success rate, duration, and MTTR metrics; alert on repeated failures and abnormal duration to team channel/on-call with weekly trend review. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-145 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Retention & Cleanup) | What retention policy is required for logs, test evidence, and artifacts, and what automated cleanup rules apply? | Keep release logs/test evidence for minimum 365 days, non-release artifacts for 90 days, and enforce automated cleanup jobs with exceptions for active investigations. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-146 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline DR) | What disaster-recovery expectations apply for pipeline operations (critical pipeline restore RTO, backup/export strategy, access recovery)? | Critical deployment pipelines require documented restore procedure with RTO <= 4 hours, regular backup/export of pipeline configuration, and emergency access recovery runbook. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-147 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline Change Control) | What review and approval workflow is required for pipeline YAML/template changes (required reviewers, CODEOWNERS, change windows)? | Pipeline/template changes require PR with CODEOWNERS enforcement, at least two reviewers including a DevOps owner; production-impacting changes follow approved change window. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-129 | LOW | Phase 2 (Promotion Strategy) | Is deployment promotion required via immutable artifacts from Dev to Test to Prod, or are direct environment deployments acceptable? | Promotion must use immutable artifacts across Dev, Test, and Prod; direct environment rebuilds are not allowed for production deployments. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-130 | LOW | Phase 2 (Sustainability) | Should Azure Well-Architected sustainability guidance be mandatory for this project, and if yes, which measurable controls apply? | Yes. Apply sustainability guardrails: right-sized SKUs, non-prod auto-shutdown, storage lifecycle policies, and quarterly review of carbon/cost optimization opportunities. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-131 | HIGH | Phase 2 (DevOps Pipelines) | Must all Azure DevOps pipelines be defined as YAML in-repo with Classic pipelines prohibited except break-glass? | Yes. YAML-only in-repo. Classic pipelines disabled; break-glass requires incident ticket, 24-hour timebox, and YAML back-port within 1 business day. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-132 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Pipeline Architecture) | What reusable pipeline template strategy is required (extends/templates, central library, and version pinning rules)? | Use reusable YAML templates from a central templates repo; consuming pipelines pin template versions by tag/commit and update through reviewed pull requests. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-133 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Trigger Strategy) | What mandatory trigger model applies for CI, PR validation, path filters, and scheduled runs? | CI on main and release/*; PR validation on all protected branches; path filters per service; nightly scheduled full regression and security scans. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-134 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Branch Protection) | Which required checks and branch policies must pass before merge (build, tests, security, approvals)? | Require passing build, unit tests, and security gates; 1 CODEOWNER approval; no direct pushes; branch must be up to date before merge. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-123 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Observability Baseline) | Which mandatory alerts, action groups, telemetry categories, and retention periods must be deployed by default via Bicep? | Deploy diagnostic settings for all supported resource logs/metrics to central Log Analytics, with Sev1/Sev2 action groups and default retention of 30 days hot plus 365 days archive. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-125 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Performance Baselines) | Which performance/capacity baselines (autoscale min/max, quota reservations, perf test triggers) are mandatory before production promotion? | Define per-workload autoscale min/max and quota reservations; run performance validation before production and block promotion on unexplained p95 latency regression above 10%. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-126 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Policy Exemptions) | What policy exemption workflow is allowed (owner, business justification, expiry, review cadence, and auto-expiry behavior)? | Exemptions require named owner, business justification, compensating controls, and max 90-day expiry; monthly review is mandatory and expired exemptions are auto-revoked. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-127 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Decommissioning) | What IaC-driven decommissioning standard is required for archival, retention, data destruction, and evidence collection? | Decommission via approved IaC change only, with retention/legal-hold check, required backup/export where applicable, secure data destruction, and stored evidence of completion. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-128 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Operations Ownership) | What is the required runbook/on-call ownership model for incident response and post-incident action tracking? | Each service must have primary and secondary owners, on-call rota, and versioned runbooks; Sev1/Sev2 incidents require postmortem and tracked corrective actions with due dates. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-124 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Cost Guardrails) | Which mandatory cost controls apply (budgets, anomaly alerts, non-prod schedules, SKU caps), and who can approve overrides? | Require budgets with 50/80/100% alerts, non-prod auto-shutdown schedules, and approved SKU allowlists; override approval requires Engineering Manager plus FinOps owner. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-112 | HIGH | Phase 2 (IaC Standards) | Which minimum/target versions are mandated for Bicep, Azure CLI, and deployment tooling, and what is the upgrade cadence? | Pin Bicep and Azure CLI via CI tool manifests/global config; allow patch roll-forward only; review and uplift versions monthly with quarterly minor-version refresh. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-113 | HIGH | Phase 2 (IaC Quality Gates) | Which mandatory pre-deploy checks must pass (bicep build, lint, policy-as-code validation, security scan), and which checks are blocking? | Blocking gates: `bicep build`, `bicep lint`, policy compliance validation, IaC security scan, and `what-if` drift review; any failed gate blocks deployment. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-114 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Secrets & Identity) | Must all pipelines use workload identity federation and managed identities only (no client secrets), and what exceptions are allowed? | Yes. Use workload identity federation and managed identities only; client secrets are prohibited by default and allowed only via time-bound security waiver with compensating controls. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-115 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Secret Handling) | What is the hard rule for secret delivery (Key Vault references only, no secrets in parameter files), and how are violations prevented? | Secrets must be sourced from Key Vault or equivalent secret store; no plaintext secrets in Bicep/parameters/repo; CI secret scanning and policy checks block violations. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-117 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Scope Model) | What are the enforced deployment boundaries across management group, subscription, and resource group scopes? | Management group scope is used for policy/guardrails, subscription scope for shared platform resources, and resource-group scope for workload deployments; cross-scope changes require platform-owner approval. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-119 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Drift Control) | How often must drift detection run, what is the remediation SLA, and who approves/executes reconciliation? | Run automated drift detection daily and before every production deployment; remediate critical drift within 24 hours and non-critical within 5 business days, approved by service owner. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-120 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Break-Glass Governance) | What controls govern break-glass actions (authorized roles, time limits, audit evidence, mandatory IaC back-port timeline)? | Break-glass access is limited to named on-call/platform roles, max 24-hour elevation, ticketed audit evidence required, and mandatory IaC back-port within 1 business day. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-121 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Reliability Targets) | What explicit SLO/SLA, RTO, and RPO targets are required per workload tier? | Define tiers: Tier 1 SLO 99.9%, RTO <= 1 hour, RPO <= 15 minutes; Tier 2 SLO 99.5%, RTO <= 4 hours, RPO <= 1 hour; Tier 3 SLO 99.0%, RTO <= 24 hours, RPO <= 24 hours. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-122 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Backup & Restore) | Which resources require backups, what retention is required, and how frequently must restore tests be executed and evidenced? | All stateful data services require automated backups with policy-based retention (min 35 days operational plus long-term as required); perform documented restore tests at least quarterly. | 2026-03-07 |
